case_title: HAMZAH AL-KORDY VS. KANSAS FOOD MARKET ET AL

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING THAT THE EXCEPTION TO THE PRIVILEGE (CIVIL CODE 47(B)(2)) APPLIES TO THIS CASE. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE AN INDEPENDENT CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FALSE IMPRISONMENT BASED ON DEFENDANTS' ACTIONS TO PREVENT PLAINTIFF FROM LEAVING THE STORE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302 =(302/SRB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-16-2015 Continued To Jan-13-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KANSAS FOOD MARKET, AHMED NASSER, AHMAD NASSER, RAED NASSER, AND ASHRAF NASSER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained. Plaintiff has failed to allege facts which show that the exception to the privilege (Civil Code section 47(b)(2) applies to this case. Plaintiff's inclusion of statements in the First Amended Complaint about the surveillance video and the broken bottle does not make Defendants' statements that Plaintiff was armed, dangerous, and in the process of robbing the store unprivileged. Furthermore, under California law, reports to police of suspected criminal activities, even false reports made with malice, are absolutely privileged and may not serve as predicates for tort claims, except the tort of malicious prosecution. (See Lauter v. Anoufrieva (2009) 642 F.Supp.2d 1060; West's Ann.Cal.Civ.Code Sec. 47.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoena Duces Tecum Of Defendant Kansas Food Markets Records From The Department Of Alcoholic Beverage Control; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Victoria E. Townsend In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KANSAS FOOD MARKET, AHMED NASSER, AHMAD NASSER, RAED NASSER, ASHRAF NASSER'S Motion To Quash Plaintiffs Subpoena Duces Tecum Of Defendant Kansas Food Markets Records From The Department Of Alcoholic Beverage Control is granted. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Sanford Kinglsey. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2015 Continued To Jun-03-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-09-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS KANSAS FOOD MARKET, AHMED NASSER, AHMAD NASSER, RAED NASSER, AND ASHRAF NASSER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege facts showing that the exception to the privilege (Civil Code 47(b)(2)) applies to this case. Plaintiff also has leave to allege an independent cause of action for false imprisonment based on defendants' actions to prevent plaintiff from leaving the store. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302.sb
